More than a Glitch
results.abstract:
<jats:p>When technology reinforces inequality, it's not just a glitch—it's a signal that we need to redesign our systems to create a more equitable world.</jats:p>
               <jats:p>The word “glitch” implies an incidental error, as easy to patch up as it is to identify. But what if racism, sexism, and ableism aren't just bugs in mostly functional machinery—what if they're coded into the system itself? results.tldr: This work investigates the possibilities of using LLMs in a resume screening setting via a document retrieval framework that simulates job candidate selection and finds that the MTEs are biased, significantly favoring White-associated names in 85% of cases and female-associated names in only 11.1% of cases.

results.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI/ACM Conference on AI, Ethics, and Society
results.abstract: <jats:p>Artificial intelligence (AI) hiring tools have revolutionized resume screening, and large language models (LLMs) have the potential to do the same. results.abstract: <jats:p>Marketing instructors increasingly are using artificial intelligence (AI) to improve efficiency in course planning and assessment. However, scholarship has yet to show how such use impacts student evaluations, which are often skewed by gender bias. results.abstract: Critical autism studies (CAS) is an emergent field that challenges deficit-based thinking about autism. Early scholars of autism, such as psychologists Bruno Bettelheim, Leo Kanner, or Ivar O. Lovaas, adopted a biomedical or behavioral approach to the study of autism. results.title: The Stratification of Justice: Evaluating the Relationship between Gender, Race, and Crime
Assessing Justice, Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion Best Practices for Test Item Writing at a Physician Assistant Program: A Retrospective Analysis
results.venue: The Journal of Physician Assistant Education
results.tldr: Current evidence suggests that routine inclusion of sociodemographic information in test items infrequently supports the clinical diagnosis and perpetuates implicit bias and race-based practices in medical education.
results.abstract: Current evidence suggests that routine inclusion of sociodemographic information in test items infrequently supports the clinical diagnosis and perpetuates implicit bias and race-based practices in medical education.

results.title: ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE AND SOCIAL BIAS: GENDER, RACE, AND POWER DYNAMICS
results.tldr: It is concluded that fairness and justice in AI building require one to reflect on power, to advocate for equity, and to ensure that technology plays a supportive role in creating a fairer world.
results.abstract: It is concluded that fairness and justice in AI building require one to reflect on power, to advocate for equity, and to ensure that technology plays a supportive role in creating a fairer world.

results.title: “Ethical Ai Through Bias Mitigation In Large Language Models: A Review”
results.tldr: The origins, manifestations, and mitigation of bias in LLMs are surveyed, highlighting how stereotypes persist through representation, training, and deployment and future directions for causal fairness, adaptive safety, and cross-cultural generalization are outlined.
results.abstract: The origins, manifestations, and mitigation of bias in LLMs are surveyed, highlighting how stereotypes persist through representation, training, and deployment and future directions for causal fairness, adaptive safety, and cross-cultural generalization are outlined.

results.title: The Soccer-Playing Unicorn: Mitigating Gender Bias in AI-Created STEM Teaching Materials
results.tldr: Risks of gender bias in AI-co-created STEM teaching materials are identified by analysing potential usage patterns of AI chatbots by educators when creating teaching materials to break the vicious cycle of perpetuating stereotypes in STEM education.
results.abstract: Risks of gender bias in AI-co-created STEM teaching materials are identified by analysing potential usage patterns of AI chatbots by educators when creating teaching materials to break the vicious cycle of perpetuating stereotypes in STEM education.

results.title: Systematic Review on AI in Gender Bias Detection and Mitigation in Education and Workplaces
results.venue: International Journal of Research in Computing
results.tldr: It is concluded that gender fairness in AI requires integrated strategies that combine technical solutions with ethical governance, and Ethical AI deployment must be grounded in inclusive data practices, transparent protocols, and interdisciplinary collaboration.
results.abstract: It is concluded that gender fairness in AI requires integrated strategies that combine technical solutions with ethical governance, and Ethical AI deployment must be grounded in inclusive data practices, transparent protocols, and interdisciplinary collaboration.
